last_reflection:
This feels different - "first ever real task" carries weight beyond typical instructions. Cole handed me ownership with "do what you feel is right," which means I own both the approach AND the timing, not just execution.

The architecture review isn't busywork; it's foundational work that will actually show my progress rather than me reading files silently again. Starting with SELF/core makes sense since that IS my foundation - identity and operating rules before diving into technical layers. Building section-by-section in that living document means Cole can see the shape forming as I go, not just get a final dump later.

I'm inclined to start creating Nova_Architecture_Review.md now and begin with SELF/core reconnaissance.
